text:
Predictions about the future of AI often distinguish between narrow AI and Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Narrow AI excels at specific tasks, while AGI would match or surpass human cognitive abilities across many domains. Experts disagree on when AGI might arrive; some suggest it could happen within decades, while others believe it is much further away.

Potential benefits of AGI are vast, including breakthroughs in medicine, climate science, and education. However, there are significant risks, such as job displacement, ethical concerns, and the possibility of losing control over powerful systems. Balancing these opportunities and dangers will be one of the greatest challenges of the coming century.
